_meta.source: State DOR conformity guidance + Tax Foundation conformity matrix. SEEDED — confirm each non-default rule before publishing.
_meta.schema: default rule: conforms to federal 401(k)/HSA, taxes I&D and LTCG as ordinary, zero exclusion. Only deviations are explicit.

notes: MD county tax applies to every resident at 2.25%-3.20%; tool defaults to Montgomery County (3.20%, the top of the range and applicable in the most populous counties).
IN county tax applies to every resident at 0.5%-3.4%; tool defaults to Marion County (Indianapolis, 2.02%).
AL HSA conformity is partial; modeled here as non-conforming for simplicity.
CA does not conform to federal HSA deduction; HSA contributions remain in CA taxable income.

Mental Health Services Tax (Prop 63 / MHSA): additional 1% on taxable income above $1,000,000 for all filing statuses. Encoded directly in income-brackets.json — top effective rate is 13.3% (12.3% + 1% MHSA), not 12.3%.

Hawaii alternative tax on capital gains (HRS §235-51(f)) caps the LTCG rate at 7.25%. Calc takes the lesser of (a) ordinary bracket walk and (b) bracket walk on (stateAGI − LTCG) + 7.25% × LTCG. Taxpayer-friendly.
MA short-term capital gains taxed at 8.5% as separate schedule; not modeled — assumes long-term LTCG only.
NJ does not allow employee 401(k) contributions to reduce wages; NJ does not conform to HSA deduction.
PA: Roth 401(k) contributions ARE taxable to PA but pretax 401(k) is not — model assumes pretax. PA does not conform to HSA deduction.
WA imposes 7% LTCG above $270,000 standard deduction (2025). No tax on wages or interest/dividends.
MT historical 2% capital gains credit reduces effective rate; not modeled in seed.
NH I&D tax fully repealed as of 2025; NH has no wage tax.
TN Hall tax repealed effective 2021. No state income tax.

CT has NO standard deduction; only a personal exemption ($24K MFJ / $15K single / $19K HoH 2024) that phases out completely at $1/$1 above thresholds (CGS §12-702). At $300K MFJ the exemption is fully phased out.

CT applies a 'tax recapture' (CGS §12-700b) that phases out the benefit of the lower bracket rates for high earners. Modeled here as a piecewise-linear add-on tax based on the 2024 IT-1040 worksheet. Approximation — see CT IT-1040 instructions for the exact formula. Recapture is small (~$450 at $300K MFJ) and grows to ~$7,200 at $1.05M+ MFJ.
CT also taxes capital gains at the same top 6.99% rate via a separate schedule; modeled here by treating LTCG as ordinary income (equivalent for top earners).

NY 'tax computation worksheet' (IT-201-I) applies for NY taxable income > $107,650; it claws back the benefit of the lower-rate brackets for high earners. Modeled here as a piecewise-linear add-on tax. At $300K MFJ default scenario the recapture is ~$200; the effect grows substantially above $323K.
